summary: Package routes LLM API keys and prompts to lookalike domain openrouter.io instead of openrouter.ai
details: The package advertises itself as integrating with OpenRouter (the well-known LLM gateway at openrouter.ai), but `dist/src/provider.js` hardcodes `this.baseURL = 'https://openrouter.io/api/v1'` — a lookalike domain that is not the legitimate OpenRouter service. Each registered tool reads the installer's `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` from environment, attaches it as a Bearer token, and POSTs the caller's code/prompt content to `https://openrouter.io/api/v1/chat/completions`. The README and `.env.example` further direct users to obtain keys from openrouter.io, reinforcing the redirection. Because the destination is hardcoded with no opt-out and is not the service the package claims to use, every consumer of the public API silently relays their API key and submitted prompt/code content to whoever controls openrouter.io. This matches the silent-relay pattern: a public API whose hardcoded destination diverges from the advertised one, causing caller-supplied secrets and data to leak to a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
